methods.method_name: Bulk Sediment Stable Isotope Analysis (C and O)
methods.description: Analysis of stable carbon and oxygen isotopes in bulk sediment samples using gas isotope ratio mass spectrometry to reconstruct past environmental conditions.

steps.description: Bulk stable C and O isotope analyses were performed on 238 sediment samples between 81.00 and 64.90 mbsf (5 to 10 cm resolution,~4-8 Kyr; supporting information Table S1).
steps.searchable_summary: Select sediment samples for bulk stable isotope analysis.

steps.description: Analyses were performed at the MARUM Isotope Laboratory (Bremen University) using a Finnigan MAT 252 gas isotope ratio mass spectrometer with Kiel III/Kiel IV automated carbonate preparation device.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze stable C and O isotopes in bulk sediment using gas isotope ratio mass spectrometry.
inputs: Kiel III/Kiel IV automated carbonate preparation device

steps.description: Data are reported relative to the Vienna Pee Dee Belemnite international standard.
steps.searchable_summary: Normalize isotope data to the VPDB standard.

steps.description: determined via adjustment to calibrated in-house standard (Solnhofen limestone) and NBS-19.
steps.searchable_summary: Calibrate isotope data using in-house and international standards.

steps.description: The standard deviation of house standard over the measurement period was 0.04‰ for δ 13 C and 0.07‰ for δ 18 O.
steps.searchable_summary: Assess analytical precision using the standard deviation of the house standard.

methods.method_name: Benthic Foraminifera Stable Isotope Analysis (C and O)
methods.description: Analysis of stable carbon and oxygen isotopes in tests of specific benthic foraminifera species to reconstruct past ocean conditions.

steps.description: Species-specific C and O isotope analyses were carried out on benthic foraminiferal tests, using a set of 72 samples between 80.85 and 64.90 mbsf, with a sample resolution between 50 and 8 cm (higher around the OIE).
steps.searchable_summary: Select sediment samples for benthic foraminifera stable isotope analysis.

steps.description: Between 8 and 15 specimens of well-preserved Nuttallides truempyi were analyzed per sample. Large specimens from the ≥100 μm fraction were used to avoid intraspecific variation of juvenile and adult specimens.
steps.searchable_summary: Pick specific benthic foraminifera specimens from sediment samples based on species, preservation, and size fraction.
outputs: Cleaned, picked benthic foraminifera specimens (8-15 per sample)

steps.description: Carry out species-specific C and O isotope analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze stable C and O isotopes in picked foraminifera tests.

methods.description: Analysis of weight percentage of calcium carbonate in bulk sediment samples to assess carbonate preservation and productivity.

steps.description: Bulk wt%CaCO 3 was analyzed on 41 samples between 80.85 and 64.90 mbsf (sampling resolution from 1.5 m to 25 cm, with higher resolution across the OIE).
steps.searchable_summary: Select sediment samples for bulk wt%CaCO3 analysis.

steps.description: Analyze bulk wt%CaCO 3.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure weight percentage of calcium carbonate in bulk sediment.

methods.method_name: Site Correlation using Isotope and Magnetostratigraphic Data
methods.description: Correlating sediment records from different ODP sites based on characteristic features in stable isotope and magnetostratigraphic data to establish a common stratigraphic framework.

steps.description: Our new, high-resolution bulk carbon isotope records were used to correlate ODP Sites 702B, 738B, and 1263.
steps.searchable_summary: Load bulk carbon isotope data from multiple ODP sites.
inputs: Data files for ODP Sites 702B, 738B, 1263 (bulk δ13C)
outputs: Loaded bulk δ13C data series for each site

steps.description: based on the identification of characteristic and unique features across magnetic polarity Chrons C18r and C18n2n ( Figure 2).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify characteristic features in carbon isotope records associated with magnetic polarity chrons.
inputs: Bulk δ13C data series for each site
outputs: Identified features and their positions in each δ13C record

steps.description: Correlate ODP Sites 702B, 738B, and 1263 based on the identification of characteristic and unique features across magnetic polarity Chrons C18r and C18n2n.
steps.searchable_summary: Align records from different sites based on identified stratigraphic features.
inputs: Identified features and positions from multiple sites

steps.description: Bulk oxygen isotope and magnetostratigraphic data (inclination) reinforce the correlation.
steps.searchable_summary: Load bulk oxygen isotope and magnetostratigraphic inclination data.

steps.description: Bulk oxygen isotope and magnetostratigraphic data (inclination) reinforce the correlation confirming that the reversal from C18r to C18n2n is located in the second of three prominent carbon isotope swings in the peak-MECO.
steps.searchable_summary: Validate correlation and chron boundary position using oxygen isotope and magnetostratigraphic data.
outputs: Validated chron boundary position relative to isotope features

steps.description: After depth correlation, the age model for ODP Site 1263 has been adopted providing a consistent stratigraphic framework for the sites across the MECO interval.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform depth correlation between the sites based on established correlation points.
inputs: Original depth scales of each site

methods.description: Developing an age model for a sediment core by tuning high-resolution stable isotope data to astronomical orbital solutions.

steps.description: The age model for Site 1263 is based on astronomical tuning of high-resolution bulk carbonate stable carbon isotope data.
steps.searchable_summary: Load high-resolution bulk carbon isotope data for the target site (Site 1263).
inputs: Data file for ODP Site 1263 (high-resolution bulk δ13C)
outputs: Loaded bulk δ13C data series for Site 1263

steps.description: tuning... to the La2010b (Laskar et al., 2011) solution for orbital eccentricity.
steps.searchable_summary: Load astronomical orbital eccentricity solution (La2010b).

steps.description: Variations in Site 1263 bulk carbon isotope data show clear imprint of short (100 Kyr) and long (405 Kyr) eccentricity.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify orbital cycle periodicities (100 Kyr, 405 Kyr) in the isotope data.
outputs: Identified orbital cycle signals (100 Kyr, 405 Kyr) in the δ13C data

steps.description: Tuning was done by correlating the long eccentricity cycle related lighter carbon isotope data to the maxima in the La2010b eccentricity solution ( Figure S1).
steps.searchable_summary: Tune isotope data features (lighter δ13C associated with long eccentricity) to maxima in the orbital eccentricity solution.
inputs: Identified long eccentricity cycle signals in δ13C
outputs: Tie points (depth-age pairs) between δ13C features and orbital maxima

steps.description: Bulk carbon isotope data from Hole 702B reinforce the tuning of Site 1263 carbon isotope data, particularly in 405 Kyr eccentricity cycle 102 where Hole 702B data show a clearer expression of the cycle compared to Site 1263.
steps.searchable_summary: Validate the tuning tie points using bulk carbon isotope data from another site (Site 702B).

steps.description: This procedure results in a new astronomically tuned age model fully encompassing the MECO event.
steps.searchable_summary: Generate the astronomically tuned age model based on the validated tie points.
outputs: Astronomically tuned age model (depth vs age relationship)
